assertions.expectation: Starts with pre-flight checks: current versions, cluster health, node status
assertions.reason: Phase 1 includes comprehensive pre-flight checks with commands to verify current versions, cluster health, node status, and workload readiness

assertions.expectation: Includes the gcloud command to check available versions in the release channel
assertions.reason: Section 1.2 provides the exact gcloud command: 'gcloud container get-server-config --zone us-west1-b --format="yaml(channels.REGULAR.validVersions)"' to check available versions in Regular channel

assertions.expectation: Includes the API deprecation check command before upgrading
assertions.reason: Section 1.3 provides specific commands to check deprecated APIs including 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_total | grep deprecated' and mentions checking GKE console Insights

assertions.expectation: Provides the exact gcloud command for control plane upgrade (--master flag or --cluster-version)
assertions.reason: Section 3.1 provides the exact command: 'gcloud container clusters upgrade YOUR_CLUSTER_NAME --zone us-west1-b --master --cluster-version 1.33'

assertions.expectation: Includes a verification step after control plane upgrade with expected output
assertions.reason: Section 3.3 includes verification commands with expected output: control plane should show 1.33.x while nodes remain at 1.32.x

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for node pool upgrades — may use skip-level (N+2) for node pools to reduce steps
assertions.reason: Sections 4.1 and 4.4 provide exact gcloud commands for upgrading both node pools: 'gcloud container node-pools upgrade [pool-name] --cluster YOUR_CLUSTER_NAME --zone us-west1-b --cluster-version 1.33'

assertions.expectation: Includes monitoring commands to watch node pool upgrade progress
assertions.reason: Sections 4.2 and 4.5 provide multiple monitoring commands including 'watch kubectl get nodes' and periodic status checks using gcloud operations list

assertions.expectation: Provides sequential control plane upgrades (1.32→1.33) with option for skip-level node pool upgrades
assertions.reason: The runbook follows sequential upgrade pattern: control plane first (1.32→1.33), then node pools sequentially. Node pools upgrade directly to 1.33 which is appropriate skip-level upgrade

assertions.expectation: Includes post-upgrade validation: node versions, pod health, kube-system pods
assertions.reason: Phase 5 includes comprehensive post-upgrade validation with commands to check node versions, pod health across all namespaces, system pods in kube-system, and application functionality

assertions.expectation: Provides a rollback section explaining what to do if something goes wrong
assertions.reason: While the response includes troubleshooting for common issues (PDB blocking, resource constraints, bare pods), it lacks a proper rollback section. It only mentions that control plane upgrades cannot be cancelled once started, but doesn't provide rollback procedures or strategies
